body.title: Decide fate of 3 never-built day-close helper scripts
body.intention: I intend that the day-close/audit functionality dropped when removing never-built script steps is either rebuilt as real tooling or consciously retired, not silently lost.
body.description:
**Problem:** day-close + day-close-audit + audit-debris referenced 3 scripts that never existed (github_sync.py = GitHub ticket backup, docs_sync.py = docs-DB sync, validate_files.py = file-placement validation). They errored every run; the steps were removed in the skills-correctness sweep. Decide per-capability: rebuild as real tooling, or retire the intent.

**Affected files:** skills/day-close, skills/day-close-audit, skills/audit-debris (steps already removed); potential new devlab/claudecode/ scripts if rebuilt.
**Design rules:** none apply.
**Scope boundary:** IN — decide+implement-or-retire each of the 3. OUT — the rest of the skills cluster.
**Test plan:** if rebuilt, each script runs clean in day-close; if retired, no skill references it (guard covers this).
**Completion criteria:** each of github-sync / docs-sync / file-placement-validation is either implemented + wired, or explicitly retired with reason.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] datacenter_logs/<device>/<subsystem>/ is the log hierarchy. Never write to a flat — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] PATTERN-001: BaseDevice / BaseShim Lifecycle: `rollback()` must be idempotent — safe to call even if — applies: tags: pattern, pattern-001 (source: docs/design_patterns_inventory.md#PATTERN-001: BaseDevice / BaseShim Lifecycle)
…and 38 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
body.result: All 3 never-built helper-script intents RETIRED — each superseded by an existing mechanism, no capability lost, nothing rebuilt: (1) docs_sync.py (docs-DB sync) — superseded by the filesystem memory store (D-filesystem-memory-store / D-canonical-memory-consolidation); docs are grep-able JSON under devlab/runtime/memory/, there is no docs-DB to sync. (2) validate_files.py (file-placement validation) — superseded by path_moves_monitor.py (T-path-moves-monitor, closed), already wired into day-close-audit Step 18.8; it scans the git file index for misfiled artifacts. (3) github_sync.py (GitHub ticket backup) — redundant: tickets are committed files under devlab/runtime/memory/tickets/ and pushed to GitHub on every commit, so they are already cloud-backed; a separate Issues-mirror adds no backup value. Enforcement that no skill re-references them is the standing guard tests/test_skills_hygiene.py::test_no_missing_script_refs (green).
body.unproven_reason: Triage/retire-intent decision — no fresh red->green lever for a 'retire, already superseded' verdict; ongoing enforcement is the standing test_no_missing_script_refs guard, not a ticket-bound proof.
